author: potuz
content: <@586161934425128960> regarding forkchoice. The spec keeps an `Optional[boolean]` which PRysm will not ever be able to have the necessary information to recreate. We will only keep a bitlist and a count of how many votes were cast which is enough to recover the sum which is what's needed for `should_build_on_full`. So the Endpoint I suggest it returns a bitlist and the count for the PTC votes

author: potuz
content: or something equivalent to that information but not more

author: potuz
content: hmmm, nevermind, perhaps we need to byte the bullet 🙁

author: potuz
content: <@881905303011086387> is there a spec for serialization of Optional?

author: nflaig
content: there is a spec for this, we have it in our ssz lib but the `GET /eth/v1/debug/fork_choice` already can't support ssz, it's json only, is the plan to extend that endpoint?

author: potuz
content: I want to implement something for that endpoint, currently we will keep three bitlists: (voted, timeliness, da). I believe this is overkill for this endpoint, so I propose the endpoint returns for each node simply the number of votes, number of votes for timely and number of votes for available.

author: potuz
content: This is enough to recover all needed information and it adds just 3 numbers per node

author: potuz
content: and only in the pending nodes

author: potuz
content: last time to decide if having the per-index data is useful or not. I'm leaning not

author: nflaig
content: cc <@575994543527297055> as you noted before that you wanna look into extend our fork choice endpoint

author: potuz
content: Can you look at the format?

author: potuz
content: if that's fine I'll open a PR in the APIs

author: nflaig
content: why are you exposing `yes` count only? I would think the `no` count is more interesting, or otherwise let's say we have 255 `yes`, but how would be know if 257 was `no`, or if we had missed votes?
